items.title: DC Readies $1.2 Billion Bond Sale as Credit Stabilizes Post-DOGE
items.description: Washington, DC is stepping up to borrow $1.2 billion this week at its annual municipal debt sale, tapping the market as its credit stabilizes.

items.title: Money Funds Keep Cash Closer as Fed Leaves Markets Guessing
items.description: Money‑market funds are shifting toward ultra short-term holdings and away from assets with even modest interest-rate risk as uncertainty grows over the Federal Reserve’s policy path and the near-term

items.title: Shipowners Offer Huge Bonuses to Get Crews to Sail Hormuz
items.description: One shipowner is attempting to coax crews to sail through the Strait of Hormuz by offering them an extra six months pay if they’re willing to cross the vital oil channel, offering seafarers a stark ch

items.title: The Oil Shock Isn’t Over
items.description: Releasing barrels of crude was the easy part.

items.title: US Immigration Policies Sink Mexican Grocery Chain Chedraui
items.description: President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown is causing the worst rout on the Mexican stock exchange this year.

items.title: National Bank of Canada Weighs SRT Tied to Project Finance Deals
items.description: National Bank of Canada is assessing investors’ appetite for a significant risk transfer deal, adding its name to a growing list of lenders exploring the instruments as a way to create room on their b

items.title: DoubleLine Says Higher Bond Yields to Help Fed Keep Rates Steady
items.description: DoubleLine is positioning in shorter-dated government bonds on the view that Kevin Warsh’s credibility with investors will help allow Federal Reserve officials to keep interest rates steady this year.

items.title: Angola Signs $900 Million Port Deals With Chinese Firms
items.description: Angola signed deals with Chinese investors to develop the Barra do Dande Integrated Development Free Zone outside Luanda, as the government seeks to establish a regional logistics hub.

items.title: Gilts Rattled on Andy Burnham’s First Day as Fiscal Fears Return
items.description: UK government bonds plunged after new Prime Minister Andy Burnham unnerved investors on his first day in the job over his approach to the country’s finances.

items.title: Netflix Taps US High-Grade Bond Market With $1 Billion Debt Sale
items.description: Netflix Inc. is set to raise $1 billion from a US high-grade bond sale, its first since a debut deal two years ago, as slowing sales growth fuels investor scrutiny of the company’s future.

items.title: Lionheart (CUB) Nears $400 Million Deal for Venezuela Oil Stake
items.description: Lionheart Capital is close to a $400 million deal for a stake in a Venezuelan oil field, in what would be one of the first major private investments in the country’s oil industry since the US began ea

items.title: BlackRock Eyes More Than $12 Billion Debt for Data Center
items.description: BlackRock Inc. is looking to sell more than $12 billion of bonds to help finance a Meta Platforms Inc. data center campus in El Paso, Texas, according to people with knowledge of the transaction, the

items.title: S&P Slips as Apple, Tesla Keep Broader Chipmaker Gains in Check
items.description: The S&P 500 Index erased early gains to finish lower as a decline in Apple Inc. and Tesla Inc. offset advances elsewhere. Chipmakers rebounded ahead of a deluge of earnings reports that will test whet

items.title: Canada Financials Stocks Close In on Top Spot as Energy Momentum Fades
items.description: Canada’s financial stocks took a breather Monday after a torrid four-month rally that put them on the verge of overtaking energy as the best performing sector in the equity market this year.

items.title: Goldman to Sell $2.5 Billion Preferreds Following Spread Low
items.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is selling new preferred shares Monday, just days after a metric determining risk and value in these securities hit its lowest level since the global financial crisis.

items.title: Alkegen Plans Prepackaged Bankruptcy in Deal for Fresh Cash
items.description: Alkegen has signed a restructuring deal with lenders that will inject the specialty materials maker with new money and slash its debt load by about $3.1 billion.

items.title: Latin America Outperforms as Emerging Market Currencies Gain
items.description: Emerging-market currencies gained to start the week, with Latin American names getting a boost from a drop in volatility and carry appeal.

items.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rate Path Amid US-Iran Conflict
items.description: Gold steadied after escalating US-Iran clashes over the weekend were followed by reports of mediation efforts, with traders assessing the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to co

items.title: Stocks, Bonds Fall as US-Iran Risks Whipsaw Oil: Markets Wrap
items.description: A renewed bout of oil volatility sent stocks lower, with concerns about an escalation in Middle East hostilities outweighing attempts to bring about a pause in the war.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
items.description: Oil edged up in a fraught session as traders grappled with a barrage of headlines that suggest a worsening of the Middle East conflict, as well as attempts to bring about a pause in the war.
